content.full_text:
October 22, 1976


Mr. James E. Hamilton
Deputy District Attorney
County of San Diego
County Courthouse
San Diego, California  92101

Dear Mr. Hamilton:

This letter is in response to your inquiry of September 29, 1976,
regarding Government Code Section 84303.

The Commission has not issued any opinions or regulations regarding
Section 84303, nor are any pending or anticipated in the near
future.  Our staff advises filers to report expenditures made by
an agent or independent contractor in their behalf as if made
directly by them unless the agent or contractor independently
reports the payments.  For example, it is insufficient for a
candidate to simply report a payment to a public relations firm
which has made expenditures for them.  The candidate must itemize
payments of $50 or more made by the firm to persons providing
goods or services to the campaign.

I hope this information is helpful.  If you would like further
guidance, please contact our staff with specific questions.

Sincerely,


NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Officer

NH:mak

bcc:  Jeanne

[illegible]


WORLDVIEW DAY
[illegible] organized [illegible]

[Date stamp or notation that appears to read:]
AUGUST 1969

WILLIAM H. KENNEDY
ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY
ROBERT C. PHELEN
CHIEF DEPUTY DISTRICT ATTORNEY
WAYNE A. BURCESS
CHIEF INVESTIGATOR

OFFICE OF

THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO
EDWIN L. MILLER, JR.
DISTRICT ATTORNEY

COUNTY COURTHOUSE
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101

XXXXXX3328
(714) 236-2428

September 29, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Ladies and Gentlemen:

Examination of campaign disclosure statements by this
office has revealed a large variance in the manner of
compliance with Government Code Section 84303 by the
various candidates and committees. It is difficult from
reading the section to determine how far the breakdown
of expenditures is intended to go.

1. Has the Commission issued any
regulations pertaining to Section 84303?

2. If not, does the Commission intend
to?

3. Are there any opinions, issued or
in the mill, which pertain to Section 84303?

Very truly yours,

EDWIN L. MILLER, JR.
District Attorney

By: [signed]
JAMES E. HAMILTON
Deputy District Attorney

JEH:bl

[illegible]

U

C

[illegible]
